description.content: Modifier for ACTION rolls. For each +DICE, add one dice to the dice pool of the 2D6 roll you are about to make. +1 DICE thus makes the roll a total of 3D6. Then roll all the dice and pick the two highest. If two +DICE are added, roll 4D6 and pick the two highest, and so on.

Modifier for ACTION rolls. For each -DICE, add one dice to the dice pool of the 2D6 roll you are about to make. Thus -1 DICE makes the roll a total of 3D6. Then roll all the dice and pick the two lowest. If two -DICE are added, roll 4D6 and pick the two lowest, and so on.

Covers attacking with Melee and Ranged Weapons, Dashing and any other activity where the player must roll on the Action Success Chart to make an Action. This roll can be modified by the opposing player by using BLOOD MARKERS. Each marker causes -1 DICE penalty to the roll.

Ranged attacks made with weapons that have this Keyword do not prevent a model from charging during the same activation. A charge may only be made if a single ranged attack is made with a weapon with this Keyword, regardless of any other rules that the weapon might have.
This model is a part of the Black Grail faction.
Supernatural or chemical temporary enhancement. Each marker can be converted into +1 DICE for any ACTION the model takes, or into -1 DICE when rolling injuries for this model.
Damage inflicted by attacks. Each can be converted into -1 DICE for any ACTION the wounded model takes or into +1 DICE when rolling injuries for this model.
BLOODBATH:

subcontent.content: You can roll an additional D6 on Injury rolls at the cost of 6 BLOOD MARKERS on any model, or at the cost of 3 BLOOD MARKERS if the target is Down. This means that instead of rolling 2D6 on your typical weapon, you roll 3D6 on your injury roll and add the numbers together. Weapons such as Artillery Witch’s Infernal Bomb would therefore roll 4D6 and add the results together (3D6+1D6).

description.content: Weakness brought about by plague. Each can be converted as if they were a BLOOD MARKER. If a model has at least one Infection Marker when activated, it gains +1 Infection Marker.
An item with this keyword can only be used once per battle and it is then gone.
When attacking with a weapon with this keyword, add +2 DICE to any injury rolls the weapon causes if you roll a Critical (i.e. 12+) on the Action Success Chart.
The most senior and heroic models of the warband. They have different rules for experience and advancement, and often enjoy a different weapon, armour and equipment selection.
Enemies of models with this Keyword suffer -1 DICE in Melee combat against this model. Some units are immune to this effect. Models that cause FEAR are not affected by FEAR themselves.

A model hit by a weapon will this Keyword suffers a BLOOD MARKER, in addition to any other effects of the attack. The model suffers this Blood Marker even if the attack has no other effects or is otherwise negated. Some equipment or abilities can negate the additional BLOOD MARKER caused by this Keyword.

This model is part of a Fireteam consisting of two models. All models that are part of the same Fireteam can be activated at the same time without the opponent getting their turn in between. They can take their ACTIONS in any order they wish, switching between the two models. Note that if the Activation of either member of the Fireteam forcefully ends (due a failed RISKY ACTION for example), it ends both Activations. Allies cannot be part of a Fireteam.

A model hit by a weapon will this Keyword suffers a BLOOD MARKER, in addition to any other effects of the attack. The model suffers this Blood Marker even if the attack has no other effects or is otherwise negated. Some equipment or abilities can negate the additional BLOOD MARKER caused by this Keyword.

Grenade-type weapons ignore penalties for cover and long range. They do not count towards the number of Ranged weapons a model can carry and do not have to be held in your hand at all times. A model armed with grenades can use them as many times as they wish.

If carrying a weapon, armour or equipment with this Keyword, the model cannot move/dash and shoot during its Activation, and the model cannot roll D6 and add it to the Charge move. Only one such item can be carried by a single model.
This model is a part of the Heretic Legion faction.
Due to their infernal origins, a model with this Keyword ignores any additional BLOOD MARKERS caused by the Keyword FIRE.
This model is not of natural origin but is instead constructed from non-organic elements.

Goetic Powers and Spells are marked with the keyword GOETIC (x) where the number in brackets indicates how many BLOOD MARKERS will have to be expanded to trigger the effect of the spell. These BLOOD MARKERS can be taken from any model currently taking part of the battle, be they friendly or enemy models, But they must come from models that do not have the Keyword DEMONIC or BLACK GRAIL. BLOOD MARKERs used this way are removed from the game, and the described effect of the Goetic Spell or ability then takes effect as described -no dice roll is needed. As with other Actions, each of these powers and spells can be used by the model only once per Activation, and they can be taken during any point of the Activation.

Models with this Keyword can be placed anywhere on the table out of line of sight of any enemies, but at least 8” away from the closest enemy. Deployed after all other models without this Keyword. If any infiltrators cannot be deployed according to these restrictions then those models can always be deployed in your deployment zone.
This model is a part of the New Antioch faction.
This model is a part of the Court of the Seven Headed Serpent faction.
This model is a part of the Pilgrim faction.
You can only purchase as many of this piece of equipment/weapon/armour as indicated by the number in parenthesis for your Warband. If you find more via looting/exploration, you can break this limit.
If you fail your roll on the Action Success Chart when taking this ACTION, the Activation of this model ends immediately.

A model hit by a weapon will this Keyword suffers a BLOOD MARKER, in addition to any other effects of the attack. The model suffers this Blood Marker even if the attack has no other effects or is otherwise negated. Some equipment or abilities can negate the additional BLOOD MARKER caused by this Keyword.
A model may only wear one piece of Headgear at a time.

When a model with this Keyword is targeted by an enemy’s Charge, it may immediately move D3” in any direction, except into Melee Combat. A Skirmisher may also make this move when a charging enemy model would enter Melee Combat with it during a Charge that is not targeting it. After either of these moves is resolved, the Charge continues as normal toward the original target. These moves can only be taken if the Skirmisher is not in Melee Combat and only one such move may be taken per Charge.
Enemy ranged attacks suffer -1 DICE against this target.
A model with this Keyword ignores the rules for weapons/armour/equipment with Keyword HEAVY. In addition, it may use a single 2-handed Melee weapon as a 1-handed weapon.
This model is a part of the Sultanate faction.
If a TOUGH model would be taken Out Of Action, it is knocked Down instead. After a TOUGH model has been knocked Down in this way once, it can be taken Out of Action as normal.

A weapon with BLAST (X) has an area of effect with a radius of inches indicated by the (X). If this weapon targets a model, this radius is measured from the centre of that model’s base in all directions. If this weapon targets a point on the ground, this radius is measured from that point in all directions, including vertically. If the Attack ACTION with this weapon is successful, it hits every model within this radius that the target (either model or point) has line of sight to (ie. not completely blocked by terrain).
Model always requires two hands to use this weapon, even if the model has Keyword STRONG.

Grenade-type weapons ignore penalties for cover and long range. They do not count towards the number of Ranged weapons a model can carry and do not have to be held in your hand at all times. A model armed with grenades can use them as many times as they wish.

As a GOLEM constructed by Kabbalistic knowledge, the model is exceedingly difficult to destroy. It treats Out of Action Injury results as Down unless the attack is done using the Bloodbath rule in which case Out of Action result removes the Scripture Guardian from the play as standard. It does not suffer additional Blood Markers from weapons with keyword GAS, and are not affected by the Keyword FEAR. The controlling player cannot remove BLOOD MARKERs from the Guardian by any means, though the opponent can use them as standard.
As long as this model is not taken Out of Action, add +1 DICE to any Morale tests the warband has to take.
Add one extra die to the roll and pick the lowest two results. This makes success less likely.

Dice that are added to or subtracted from Injury Rolls. If the Keyword applies to a Weapon, the dice are only added to Injury Rolls for Attacks made with it. A higher Injury Roll result means a more severe injury on the Injury Table.
Add +1 die to the Injury Roll for attacks made with this weapon. Roll 3D6 and pick the two highest results. A higher result means a more severe injury on the Injury Table.
Add -1 die to the Injury Roll for attacks made with this weapon. Roll 3D6 and pick the two lowest results. A lower result means a less severe injury on the Injury Table.
Modifiers that are applied to the final result of an Injury Roll. If the Keyword applies to a Weapon, the modifier is only applied to Injury Rolls for Attacks made with it.
Add +2 to the final result of the Injury Roll for attacks made with this weapon. A higher result means a more severe injury outcome on the Injury Table.
Subtract 1 from the final result of the Injury Roll for attacks against a model with this. A lower result means a less severe injury outcome on the Injury Table.
Subtract 2 from the final result of the Injury Roll for attacks against a model with this. A lower result means a less severe injury outcome on the Injury Table.

If a model has a piece of Battlekit with this Keyword, it will use it in the next game. When the model is deployed, say which Ranged Weapon the Battlekit will be used for — it gains the (KEYWORD) until the end of the game. The Ranged Weapon cannot have the BLAST, FIRE, GAS, or SHRAPNEL Keywords, and cannot have more than one type of AMMUNITION.

A Weapon with this Keyword reduces the target's total -INJURY MODIFIER from its Armour Characteristic, and/or any Armour or Shields it has, by 1, to a minimum of 0. For example, if a target had Standard Armour and a Trench Shield, the -INJURY MODIFIER would be lowered from -2 to -1.

When you take a Shoot ACTION with a Ranged Weapon that has this Keyword, you can make a number of Ranged Attacks with this weapon equal to X, one after another. Attacks can target different enemy models as long as they are all within 6" of each other. Any BLOOD MARKERS or BLESSING MARKERS spent only modify the Injury Roll for the Ranged Attack they are spent on.

A stipulation on a Ranged Weapon. You must purchase a Ranged Weapon with a Bayonet Lug before you can purchase a Bayonet Melee Weapon for that model. The Bayonet attaches to the firearm and is used in melee combat.

This weapon has a 3" blast radius. Every model within 3" that has Line of Sight to the target is hit on a Success or Critical Success. Friendly models within 1" of a hit enemy are also hit. Make an Injury Roll for every model hit.
When you deploy a model with this Keyword for the first time in a game, place a number of BLESSING MARKERS beside the model equal to X.
Add -1 DICE for Melee Attacks targeting a model with this Keyword, or that has a Weapon with this Keyword, if the attacker made a Charge ACTION before making the attack this Turn.

When you take a Fight ACTION with a Weapon that has this Keyword, you can make a number of Melee Attacks equal to X, one after another. The attacks can target different enemy models as long as they are all within 1" of the attacking model. Any BLOOD MARKERS spent only modify the Injury Roll for the attack they are spent on.

A model has Cover if it is in contact with a terrain piece that is at least ½" high, at least as wide as its base, and the terrain piece lies between it and the attacker in a way that partially blocks Line of Sight. Add -1 DICE to Ranged Attacks targeting a model in Cover.

When you make an Injury Roll for an attack with this Keyword, roll 3D6 and add all 3 dice together. Any +INJURY DICE or -INJURY DICE are added normally, except that you pick the 3 highest or lowest dice instead of the 2 highest or lowest.
When a Weapon with this Keyword is used to make a Ranged Attack it is automatically a Success — do not make a Success Roll. Note that this means the attack cannot achieve a Critical Success.

When moving, retreating or charging, you can measure the path through the air. The model must end on the battlefield or a terrain piece. Still take a Risky Success Roll if activated or ending on Dangerous terrain. Cannot end on Impassable terrain. Do not make an Injury Roll if this model Falls.

Requires one hand to carry and cannot be put down. A model with this Keyword can only be equipped with either a 1-Handed Weapon or a Shield — not a 2-Handed Weapon, and not both a Weapon and a Shield. It may still carry Grenades.
Ignore -INJURY DICE and -INJURY MODIFIERS for a target's Armour Characteristic, and for any Armour or Shield pieces of Battlekit that it has, for attacks that have this Keyword.
Ignore the Success Roll or Injury Roll modifier that is specified. For example, IGNORE COVER means the attack is not affected by the -1 DICE modifier for a target in Cover.
Ignore the -1 DICE modifier to the Success Roll that would normally apply when the target is in Cover.
Ignore the -1 DICE modifier to the Success Roll that would normally apply when attacking at Long Range (beyond half the weapon's range).
Ignore the +1 DICE bonus that would normally apply when the attacker is in an elevated position at least 3" higher than the target.
Ignore the specified armour modifier when making Injury Rolls against the target.

The ARMOUR PIERCING and IGNORE ARMOUR Effects do not affect any -INJURY DICE and -INJURY MODIFIERS that apply to Battlekit that has this Keyword. Any other Battlekit the target model has is affected normally.

When a model moves into contact with a Marker or terrain piece with this Keyword, the mine detonates unless the model has NEGATE MINED. Make an Injury Roll with the SHRAPNEL Keyword for the detonating model, then the Marker or terrain piece loses the MINED Keyword. Models with the FLYING Keyword only detonate a mine if they finish a move in contact with it.
A model with the NEGATE Keyword is not affected by the specified Keyword's Effect. For example, a model with NEGATE SHRAPNEL ignores the Effect of the SHRAPNEL Keyword.
This model is not affected by the FIRE Keyword's Effect — it does not receive the extra BLOOD MARKER from attacks with the FIRE Keyword.
This model is not affected by the GAS Keyword's Effect — it does not receive the extra BLOOD MARKER from attacks with the GAS Keyword.
This model is not affected by the FEAR Keyword's Effect — it does not suffer the -1 DICE penalty to Melee Attacks against models with FEAR.
This model is not affected by the HEAVY Keyword's restrictions. It can equip and use one 2-Handed Melee Weapon as if it were a 1-Handed Melee Weapon.
This model is not affected by the SHRAPNEL Keyword's Effect — it does not receive the extra BLOOD MARKER from attacks with the SHRAPNEL Keyword.

Can be used as a Melee Weapon or a Ranged Weapon, and as both in the same Activation. When used as a Ranged Weapon it uses the Range shown on its Profile and the model's Ranged Characteristic. When used as a Melee Weapon it can use the model's Ranged or Melee Characteristic and can be used as an Off-Hand Weapon.
If a model makes an attack with a Weapon that has this Keyword, its Activation ends after the ACTION that allowed the attack is completed.
When you Activate a model with this Keyword, before carrying out any ACTIONS, you can remove up to X BLOOD MARKERS from the model.

Some Weapons with BLAST also have SCATTER. If the Success Roll is a Failure, the attack scatters instead of missing. Subtract the Success Roll from 7 to find the scatter distance in inches (e.g. a roll of 4 scatters 3"). Your opponent moves the attack that many inches in a direction of their choice. There must be Line of Sight between the new point and the original target. Then determine who is hit as per BLAST rules.
Add -1 INJURY DICE to rolls for attacks made at Long Range with a Weapon that has this Keyword instead of the usual Long Range modifier (-1 DICE to hit).
A model can have a Shield and a 2-Handed Weapon if both have the Shield Combo stipulation. Normally a Shield prevents a model from carrying a 2-Handed Weapon.
